meta.title: Castle of Kruja – Krujë, Albania
meta.description: Complete travel guide to the Castle of Kruja. Learn about this historic Albanian castle, including location, views, access, and visitor information.

intro.title: Discover the Castle of Kruja
intro.description: The Castle of Kruja (Kalaja e Krujës) is a historic castle in the city of Krujë, Albania. It was the center of Skanderbeg's rebellion against the Ottoman Empire. The castle houses the Skanderbeg Museum, the National Ethnographic Museum, and the remains of the Fatih Sultan Mehmed mosque, offering visitors a deep dive into Albanian history.

visitGuide.title: Visit Tips
items: Explore the Old Bazaar of Kruja on your way up to the castle.
Don't miss the Skanderbeg Museum located inside the castle walls.
Wear comfortable shoes as the pathways are made of cobblestones.
Enjoy panoramic views of the surrounding mountains and the Adriatic Sea.

sections.title: History & Heritage
sections.content: The Castle of Kruja was built in the 5th or 6th century and became famous during the 15th century as the headquarters of Gjergj Kastrioti Skanderbeg. Under his leadership, the castle withstood three massive sieges by the Ottoman Empire, becoming a symbol of Albanian resistance.

sections.title: Location & Landscape
sections.content: Perched on a rocky hill above the city of Krujë, the castle offers a commanding view of the surrounding landscape. Its strategic location allowed defenders to monitor approaching armies and control the trade routes.

sections.title: Architecture & Surroundings
sections.content: The castle features thick defensive walls, watchtowers, and the remains of historical structures. Inside, the modern Skanderbeg Museum, designed by Enver Hoxha's daughter, blends traditional Albanian architecture with modern elements.

sections.title: Cultural Significance
sections.content: Today, the Castle of Kruja is one of Albania's most important cultural and historical landmarks. It attracts thousands of visitors annually who come to learn about the country's national hero and its rich heritage.

items.question: What are the opening hours of the Castle of Kruja?
items.answer: The castle grounds are generally open from morning until evening. The museums inside have specific visiting hours, typically from 9:00 AM to 6:00 PM.

items.question: Is there an entrance fee?
items.answer: Walking around the castle grounds is free, but there is an entrance fee for the Skanderbeg Museum and the Ethnographic Museum.

items.question: Are there guided tours available?
items.answer: Yes, guided tours are available at the museums and can provide a richer understanding of the history.

items.question: Is the site accessible for wheelchair users?
items.answer: Due to the historic nature of the site and cobblestone paths, accessibility can be challenging for wheelchair users.

hours.title: Visiting Hours
hours.bestTimeSummer: Summer: Busy but great for photography
hours.tip: Visit early in the morning to avoid the crowds and enjoy the best lighting.

transport.title: How to Get Here
transport.fromCenterDesc: About a 45-minute drive or minibus (furgon) ride from Tirana. Take the SH1 highway north, then turn onto SH38 directly to Krujë.
transport.fromStationDesc: A 10-15 minute walk up the hill through the historic Old Bazaar along the cobblestone pedestrian street to the castle entrance.
transport.fromAirportDesc: About 30-40 minutes by taxi or rental car. Take SH52 to SH1, then turn onto SH38.
transport.publicTransportDesc: Minibuses run frequently every day between Tirana (from the North Bus Terminal) and Krujë. The ride takes about 1 hour.
transport.walkingDesc: Once in Krujë town center, follow the signs for 'Kalaja' and walk through the bazaar. It is a very short distance.
transport.drivingDesc: Follow signs to Krujë; there are several paid parking lots available near the castle entrance and in the town center.

gallery.subtitle: The Beauty of Castle of Kruja

reviews.title: Visitor Reviews
reviews.declaration: Reviews can be viewed via Google Maps.
reviews.moreReviews: View more reviews on Google Maps

items.name: Sarah J.
items.text: An amazing piece of history! The views from the castle are spectacular, and the Skanderbeg Museum is very informative.

items.name: Mark T.
items.text: Walking through the Old Bazaar to get to the castle feels like stepping back in time. Highly recommend a visit.

items.name: Elena R.
items.text: Beautiful location and rich history. Wear comfortable shoes as the cobblestones can be tricky.

items.name: David L.
items.text: A must-see in Albania. The Ethnographic Museum gives a great look into traditional Albanian life.

items.name: Anna K.
items.text: Stunning architecture and incredible views. We spent hours just wandering around the castle grounds.

items.name: Tom H.
items.text: Great historical site. The museum is well laid out and the whole area is very photogenic.

officialManagement.title: About Castle of Kruja
officialManagement.text: The Castle of Kruja is a protected national monument, managed and maintained by the Ministry of Culture of Albania and the National Institute of Cultural Heritage (IKTK), preserving the rich history and heritage of Albania.
